questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a trolley track experiment to check the key physics idea. A trolley has a mass of 3 kg and a resultant force of 6 N. What is its acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration equals resultant force divided by mass.
questions.senNote: Acceleration equals resultant force divided by mass.

questions.explanation: Acceleration equals resultant force divided by mass.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a spanner turning a nut to check the key physics idea. A force of 15 N acts 0.6 m from a pivot. What is the moment?
questions.explanation: Moment equals force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot.
questions.senNote: Moment equals force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a seesaw balance problem to check the key physics idea. A force of 20 N acts 0.8 m from a pivot. What is the moment?
questions.explanation: Moment equals force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot.
questions.senNote: Moment equals force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot.

questions.explanation: Moment equals force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot.
questions.senNote: Moment equals force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a trolley collision setup to check the key physics idea. A trolley of mass 3 kg moves at 4 m/s. What is its momentum?
questions.explanation: Momentum equals mass multiplied by velocity.
questions.senNote: Momentum equals mass multiplied by velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a snowshoe pressure comparison to check the key physics idea. A force of 140 N acts on an area of 3 m^2. What pressure is produced?
questions.explanation: Pressure equals force divided by area.
questions.senNote: Pressure equals force divided by area.

questions.explanation: Pressure equals force divided by area.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a road safety lesson to check the key physics idea. A car travels on tired driver. Which answer is best?
options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
Wet roads reduce stopping distance in every case.
The thinking distance increases because reactions are slower.
questions.correctAnswer: The thinking distance increases because reactions are slower.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a driver reaction scenario to check the key physics idea. A car travels on worn tyres. Which answer is best?
options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
The braking distance increases because grip is reduced.
Higher speed always reduces braking distance.
questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because grip is reduced.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a tyre-grip comparison to check the key physics idea. A car travels on icy road. Which answer is best?
options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The braking distance increases because friction is much lower.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
Worn tyres improve the grip on the road.
questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because friction is much lower.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a braking distance discussion to check the key physics idea. A car travels on wet roads. Which answer is best?
options: The braking distance increases because friction is reduced.
Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
Reaction time has no effect on stopping distance.
questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because friction is reduced.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a road safety lesson to prepare for an exam-style question. A car travels on tired driver. Which answer is best? Choose the best answer.
options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
The brakes remove the need for friction.
The thinking distance increases because reactions are slower.
questions.correctAnswer: The thinking distance increases because reactions are slower.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a driver reaction scenario to prepare for an exam-style question. A car travels on worn tyres. Which answer is best? Choose the best answer.
options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
The braking distance increases because grip is reduced.
Ice increases grip between the tyres and road.
questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because grip is reduced.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.

The thinking distance increases because reactions are slower.
questions.correctAnswer: The thinking distance increases because reactions are slower.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics

options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
The braking distance increases because grip is reduced.
Mass has no effect on the kinetic energy to remove.
questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because grip is reduced.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a road safety lesson to test a common misconception. A car travels on icy road. Which answer is best?
options: Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.
The braking distance increases because friction is much lower.
The car stops instantly when the driver sees danger.
Braking distance is the distance before the driver reacts.
questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because friction is much lower.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

options: The braking distance increases because friction is reduced.
Only thinking distance changes; braking distance is fixed.

Thinking distance is the distance after the brakes are applied.

questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
questions.senNote: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.

questions.correctAnswer: The braking distance increases because friction is much lower.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance depends on thinking distance and braking distance. Grip and reaction time both matter.
